title: Tell El-Qulzum (Clysma)
description: The ancient mounds of Clysma, a vital port city linking the Nile to the Red Sea, featuring remains from Ptolemaic and Roman periods including ancient hydraulic systems.

title: Qasr Muhammad Ali
description: Historical royal rest house built under the Muhammad Ali dynasty for use during inspections of the shipyard and pilgrimage preparations.

title: Sidi El-Arbaeen Mosque
description: A historic mosque named after forty scholars; it served as a center of resistance during the popular defense of Suez in 1973.

title: Sidi El-Gharib Mosque
description: Dedicated to a Moroccan Sufi saint who settled in Suez to guard Islamic frontiers, considered the spiritual patron of the city.

title: Port Tewfik Memorial
description: A stone memorial erected to honor the Indian soldiers who fell during the defense of the Suez Canal in World War I.

title: Green Island (El-Gezira El-Khadra)
description: A fortified island fortress in the Gulf of Suez, site of a major commando raid during the War of Attrition.

title: Al-Mahmal Square
description: The historical gathering plaza where the ceremonial Palanquin (Mahmal) carrying the Kiswa for the Kaaba would depart for Mecca.

title: The Latin Church
description: The Church of the Good Shepherd, a testament to the cosmopolitan community brought together by the digging of the Suez Canal.

title: Old Houses of Port Tewfik
description: Colonial-style wooden villas and chalets built for Suez Canal Company employees, featuring distinctive French architectural influences.

title: Ayoun Moussa (Moses Springs)
description: Located shortly after the Ahmed Hamdi Tunnel, these springs are the traditional site where Moses turned bitter water sweet.

title: Baghla Island
description: A small strategic islet in the bay, used historically for coastal monitoring and navigational guidance for the canal entrance.

title: Al-Satati Cemetery
description: One of the oldest cemeteries in the city, containing tombs of significant local figures and reflecting the city's social history.

title: Awlad El-Sheikh Archeological Area
description: A historical area known for its shrines and traditional fabric, representing the spiritual heritage of the canal city.

title: Old Suez Train Station
description: The terminus of the first railway line in Africa (Cairo-Suez), crucial for the overland route before the canal's completion.

title: Suez Canal House
description: The iconic administrative headquarters of the Suez Canal Company at the southern entrance, offering panoramic views of the waterway.

title: Ahmed Hamdi Tunnel Memorial
description: Monument dedicated to General Ahmed Hamdi, located near the vital tunnel connecting Africa and Asia.

title: Suez National Museum
description: Opened to house artifacts ranging from prehistoric to modern times, documenting the history of the canal and city.
